title: Azure OpenAI Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Azure OpenAI for AI-driven applications.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content like stories or poems.

commands.description: Limits the response length to 150 tokens.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers for a chatbot.

commands.description: Specifies the model version to use.
commands.scenario: When you require the latest model for improved accuracy.

commands.description: Enables streaming responses for real-time applications.
commands.scenario: When building a live chat application that requires immediate feedback.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating phrases in the output.
commands.scenario: When generating diverse text outputs for content creation.

commands.description: Increases the chance of introducing new topics.
commands.scenario: When encouraging the model to explore new ideas in brainstorming sessions.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authentication.
commands.scenario: When initializing the API client to start making requests.

commands.description: Specifies the endpoint for API requests.
commands.scenario: When configuring the client to connect to your Azure OpenAI resource.

commands.description: Prevents the model from generating specific tokens.
commands.scenario: When you want to avoid certain phrases or words in the output.

commands.description: Encourages the model to provide more detailed and logical responses.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require detailed explanations.

commands.description: Instructs the model to format the response in bullet points.
commands.scenario: When generating lists or structured information.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When ensuring the application does not wait indefinitely for a response.

commands.description: Retries the request up to 3 times in case of failure.
commands.scenario: When handling transient errors in network requests.

commands.description: Sets the logging level to debug for detailed logs.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues with API requests.

commands.description: Nucleus sampling: limits the model's consideration to a subset of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches the threshold, reducing the long tail of low-probability tokens.
Enables deterministic outputs by setting a specific seed for the random number generator, essential for debugging and reproducible testing.

commands.command: System Message: 'You are a JSON-only assistant. Output valid JSON without markdown formatting.'
commands.description: Prompt engineering pattern to force structured data output and prevent the model from wrapping responses in triple backticks (```json).
Required parameter in Azure OpenAI to specify the specific model deployment name rather than just the model type.
Debugging technique: capture this unique header value to provide to Microsoft support for tracing specific failed requests in Azure logs.
Limits the model's vocabulary to the top K most likely next tokens to reduce tail-end noise and improve coherence.
Defines specific sequences where the model should cease generation, preventing the model from hallucinating subsequent turns in a conversation.
Use triple backticks (```) or XML-style tags (<example></example>) to clearly separate few-shot examples from the actual instruction to prevent prompt injection/leakage.

commands.command: Azure OpenAI Content Filter Bypass Testing
commands.description: Use the 'Hate/Violence/Self-Harm' detection headers in response to debug why a specific prompt was flagged and trigger a manual review via the Azure AI Studio.

commands.command: Token usage monitoring: usage.prompt_tokens vs usage.completion_tokens
commands.description: Track the ratio of input to output tokens to optimize cost and manage context window limits for long-running agentic workflows.
